findings.signature: as Database.Statement cast on prepare().all()/.get() results
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 Statement type assertion; Turso returns different statement interface, all casts must be reviewed

findings.signature: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
findings.notes: FTS5 virtual table existence check; Turso lacks FTS5, this gate will always return false, disabling sqlite BM25 engine path
better-sqlite3 spread-parameter binding idiom; Turso uses positional ? but binding API differs, needs adapter layer

findings.signature: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3'; function params typed Database.Database
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 type import; must be replaced with Turso/libSQL client type or abstracted behind interface
PRAGMA table_info via prepare(); Turso requires db.execute('PRAGMA table_info(...)') or query() instead of prepare+all pattern
db.exec() for DDL; Turso uses db.execute() or db.batch(); low risk since ALTER TABLE is standard SQL

findings.signature: SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
findings.notes: Reads FTS5 CREATE statement to verify isolation; Turso has no FTS5 so memory_fts won't exist, isolation check becomes vacuous

findings.signature: db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(memory_index)').all() + db.exec('ALTER TABLE ... DROP COLUMN')
findings.notes: PRAGMA via prepare() + exec() for DDL; both need Turso adapter, but DROP COLUMN is standard SQL 3.35.0+

findings.signature: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3'; safeGetDb(): Database.Database | null
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 type import; all function signatures using Database.Database need Turso-compatible type or abstraction

findings.signature: safeGetDb() delegates to vectorIndex.getDb() returning better-sqlite3 Database
findings.notes: Transitive better-sqlite3 dependency via vector-index module; migration requires vector-index to return Turso client
FTS5 string literal in type union; not a runtime dependency but signals the fallback pipeline assumes FTS5 availability

findings.signature: function gates on isMemoryFtsAvailable(database) returning boolean
findings.notes: Entire sqlite lexical engine path gated on FTS5 table existence; Turso migration forces all users to in-memory BM25 fallback
